title: artemisia camphorata var. xerophila
intro: Artemisia camphorata var. xerophila, also known as Western mugwort, is a plant species in the daisy family native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Western mugwort include relief from menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and anxiety, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Western mugwort can be prepared for medicinal use through teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
